challenges.title: Pointer Basics
challenges.description: Write a C++ program that declares an int variable and a pointer to it. Use the address-of operator (&) to assign the variable's address to the pointer. Use the dereference operator (*) to print the value through the pointer. Also print the memory address stored in the pointer. Assign your C++ code to the variable `cpp`.
challenges.test_code:
assert 'int*' in cpp.replace(' ', '') or 'int *' in cpp, 'You must declare a pointer variable (int* or int *)'
assert '&' in cpp, 'Use the address-of operator (&) to get the variable address'
assert cpp.count('*') >= 2, 'Use the dereference operator (*) to access the value through the pointer'
assert 'cout' in cpp, 'Print the value and address using cout'
hints: Dereference with *ptr to get the value, and use ptr alone to print the address

challenges.title: Dynamic Memory Allocation
challenges.description: Write a C++ program that uses `new` to dynamically allocate an array of integers. Ask the user for the array size, allocate memory with `new`, fill the array with values using a loop, print all values, then release the memory with `delete[]`. Assign your C++ code to the variable `cpp`.
challenges.test_code:
assert 'new' in cpp, 'Use the new operator to allocate dynamic memory'
assert 'delete' in cpp, 'Use delete to free the dynamically allocated memory'
assert 'delete[]' in cpp.replace(' ', '') or 'delete []' in cpp, 'Use delete[] (with brackets) to free a dynamically allocated array'
assert 'cin' in cpp, 'Use cin to get the array size from the user'
assert 'for' in cpp or 'while' in cpp, 'Use a loop to fill and print the array'
hints: Always pair new[] with delete[] to avoid memory leaks
